student.dataset_description: UCI Student Performance dataset (Open University). Predicts student final result (Pass/Fail/Distinction/Withdrawn). Contains demographic and behavioral columns. Many categorical columns with low cardinality. Student ID already removed.
must_do: encode_categorical with one_hot for nominal columns
must_not_do: Do NOT use ordinal encoding on nominal categories
student.notes: Dataset domain: education. Categorical nature: mostly nominal with low cardinality (region, highest_education, disability). High-cardinality columns: code_module and code_presentation have moderate cardinality. ML model encoding guidance: One-hot encoding is strongly preferred. KNN uses Euclidean distance — ordinal encoding of nominal features makes distance meaningless (distance between 'race=1' and 'race=3' would be 2, implying they're more different than 'race=1' and 'race=2'). One-hot preserves equal distances between categories.
